workItem.title: Honor provider Retry-After in issue retry scheduling

## Context

During a game-api rate-limit response carrying a provider retry window, Symphony retained only its generic issue retry delay and scheduled another turn in roughly 25 seconds. The provider circuit prevented the HTTP call, but the issue scheduler still woke Codex prematurely and consumed runtime resources.

## Expected outcome

Provider Retry-After becomes a lower bound for per-issue retry scheduling, stale provider delays are cleared for unrelated failures, and the installed X1 runtime matches the merged implementation.

## Scope

- Persist provider_retry_after_ms from structured tracker failures
- Use the provider window as a lower bound in retry_delay
- Clear stale provider delay for non-rate failures
- Add regression coverage for long and stale provider windows
- Publish, review, merge and reinstall the exact verified commit

## Out of scope

- Changing the global tracker polling interval
- Increasing agent concurrency
- Changing game-api rate-limit semantics

## Acceptance criteria

- A provider Retry-After longer than exponential backoff controls the issue retry time
- Shorter provider windows do not weaken the existing backoff
- A later non-rate failure cannot reuse a stale provider window
- The complete Symphony validation suite passes
- The installed X1 escript is built from the merged implementation
